data.change_summary: Validated the existing local implementation for this fix: `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` now classifies Codex `--output-last-message` file-read failures as `FailureClass::TransportFailure`, while leaving `serde_json::from_str(...)` failures classified as `FailureClass::SchemaValidationFailure`. Test coverage in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs` distinguishes the missing/unreadable last-message path from the invalid-JSON path.
outstanding_risks: The worktree contains unrelated local modifications outside this task; they were left untouched.

steps.description: Inspected the adapter and retry policy paths to confirm where Codex last-message file-read failures and JSON parse failures are classified.

Verified the adapter implementation keeps Codex last-message read errors mapped to `TransportFailure` and JSON parse errors mapped to `SchemaValidationFailure`, with matching unit-test coverage for both branches.
Ran the required Nix-based build and test commands to confirm the change set passes without altering CLI behavior.

validation_evidence: Confirmed in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` that the `tokio::fs::read_to_string(&message_path)` error branch returns `FailureClass::TransportFailure`, and the subsequent `serde_json::from_str(&last_message_text)` error branch returns `FailureClass::SchemaValidationFailure`.

Confirmed in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs` that `codex_missing_last_message_returns_transport_failure` covers the file-read failure path and `codex_invalid_last_message_returns_schema_validation_failure` covers the JSON parse failure path.
`nix develop -c cargo test` completed successfully; the full suite passed, including lib tests, CLI tests, unit tests, and doc tests.
